slices.focus: Workshop redesign: slides to hands-on packets
slices.summary: Redesigned corridor workshops from slide-deck presentations to take-home document packets with color-coded checklists and call-back sheets after weak follow-through from well-attended evening sessions. Shifted role from presenter to follow-through manager. Running one more round with minor wording fixes before evaluating whether the new format actually improves completion rates.
open_loops: Whether the new format actually improves completion rates needs next-round data to validate
Need to learn how residents actually use the checklist at home during follow-up calls
Evening events and irregular eating may aggravate acid reflux, needs monitoring
decisions: Keep core structure for one more round, only fix confusing wording to preserve data comparability
Add next-action field to call-back sheets for more targeted follow-up calls
Track three metrics: first-step completion within one week, stall points, and restart callback count
